spec_match.rationale: Uses `--angle / 1deg` to strip the unit and reuse it across deg/percent/unitless — exact spec application.

interactive.rationale: Two interactive markers — one slider and one supporting element. Drives a needle, bar, opacity but the control surface is light.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises one custom property driving three units; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: What if the angle wraps around 360deg? What about negative angles? The slider range matters and isn't shown.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Invariant 3 — chromestatus.com/feature/4740780497043456 must be in see-also.

openQuestions.title: Show negative and wraparound angles
openQuestions.detail: Extend the slider to negative values and >360deg. Does the percent bar saturate? Does opacity clamp? Visitors learn the boundary semantics.

openQuestions.title: Add the calc() formula above each derived value
openQuestions.detail: Show the actual calc expression for the bar (`calc(var(--angle) / 360deg * 100%)`) and for opacity. Currently the linkage is implicit; surfacing the formulas teaches the syntax.

summary: Lovely conceptual demo — one property, three derived units. Add a chromestatus link, expose the formulas, and show wraparound to graduate it.
